mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate signals tightening monetary policy that raises discount rates and equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following month-over-month FEDFUNDS increases are positive in more than 55% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0%, further month-over-month increases in the unemployment rate signal deteriorating labor demand that reduces corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and industrial production indicate a contraction in both final demand and manufacturing output, which jointly amplifies recession risk and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint decline signal fails to produce negative 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered quarters, or single-series effects explain the prediction equally well (interaction term p-value > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During periods when the federal funds rate is below 1%, month-over-month increases in industrial production signal a supply-led recovery that is not yet constrained by monetary tightening, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 1.0%, month-over-month INDPRO increases are followed by negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in real retail sales exceeding 1.5% signals a sharp contraction in consumer spending that reduces corporate revenue expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Sharp RSAFS declines are followed by positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals accelerating manufacturing activity that boosts earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: High INDPRO growth months are followed by negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment combined with falling retail sales indicates a labor market disconnect where hiring runs ahead of consumption, compressing profit margins and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of PAYEMS increase and RSAFS decrease fails to produce negative 60-day returns in more than 60% of instances, or either series alone produces equal or better directional accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: During periods when the federal funds rate is below 0.25%, quarter-over-quarter increases in real GDP signal that monetary stimulus is successfully transmitting to aggregate demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 increases are followed by negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.
